skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: The dot plots represent two data sets A and B, both symmetric and with an odd number of values. Data set A has values 20, 21, 22, 22, 23, 24, 24, 25, 26 (one dot each except 22 and 24 have one each as well). Data set B has values 20, 20, 21, 22, 22, 23, 24, 24, 25, 26, 26. Both distributions are symmetric about 23. Consider the two statements: I. The median of A equals the median of B. II. The standard deviation of A is greater than the standard deviation of B. Which of the following is true?
rationale: Both distributions are symmetric with odd counts; the median equals the value at the center, which is 23 for each set. So statement I is true. Standard deviation measures spread from the mean: data set B has more values at the extremes (20 and 26 each appear twice) than A does, so B has a larger standard deviation. That means statement II (A > B) is false. Only I is true.
